abstract: Audit a live URL on multiple mobile viewports, locate the primary CTA, measure how far below the fold it renders, and produce a surgical CSS patch that pulls it above the fold without redesigning the page. Uses Playwright for headless measurement and CSS injection to render a before/after comparison on the live DOM. Best for stakeholder feedback like 'the button is below the fold on mobile' or 'lots of unused vertical space.' No source-code access required — works on any public URL.
methodology: Capture mobile screenshots and DOM tree at 3 breakpoints (375/390/360)
Walk hero DOM, pin top contributors to hero height
Write surgical CSS patch using real class names
Inject patch on live DOM via Playwright addStyleTag, re-measure
Hand off with measured proof (before/after table + screenshots)
common_culprits: min-height: 100vh on hero forcing viewport-fill on mobile
Decorative imagery in DOM before headline + CTA
Fixed paddings that don't step down on mobile
fix_priority_order: Remove or zero out min-height on mobile
Reorder DOM with flex order so text + CTA come first
deliverables: Markdown report with measured before/after table
case_studies: references/impeccable-style/ — vendor product page where primary CTA moved from y=1011px to y=427px on iPhone SE (584px above-fold improvement)
